title: CodeMirror comment gutter
description:
Add a custom CodeMirror gutter showing existing comments on the lines they're anchored to.

Behaviour:
- A small marker (dot or chip) in the gutter on each line that has at least one open or relocated comment. Resolved comments don't render markers.
- Click on a marker: open the comments sidebar (if closed), filter or jump to the entry for that line, and focus that line in the editor.
- Click on an empty gutter cell (any line): open the Add Comment modal pre-filled with that line as the anchor (currently this is bound to the header action that reads the cursor's current line — gutter clicks would be more direct).
- Multiple comments per line should be supported in the marker count.

Implementation hints:
- CodeMirror 6 `gutter` extension; provide a custom `gutterMarker` per line based on the comments store keyed by current doc.
- Reactive update when comments-store changes (add/remove/status change).
- Marker style follows status colours used in the sidebar (open=blue, relocated=yellow).

## Context
User flagged this as important during the auth wrap-up — said the gutter is the natural UX for line-anchored comments.
close_reason: shipped: CodeMirror gutter with status-colored markers (blue=open, yellow=relocated), click on marker opens sidebar + focuses line, click on empty cell opens AddCommentModal pre-filled with the line. Resolved/orphaned hidden.

title: Path-based permissions
description:
Implement the permissions table (subject_type, subject_id, path_glob, permission) and enforce on read/write/comment endpoints per doc §19. Subjects: users, groups, roles. Permissions: read, write, comment. Path globs match against the document path.

## Context
M7 auth plan deferred this — schema is sketched in doc §6. Needs design pass on glob matching semantics + admin UI for managing entries.
close_reason: shipped: path_glob/subject_type/permission rules + admin UI + replace-defaults semantics, doublestar globs, fuzzy implication. Out of scope for v1: deny rules, more-specific-wins precedence.

title: Password reset flow over SMTP
description:
Now that SMTP infrastructure is in place (bd-2vu), wire the actual password-reset flow.

Schema:
- New table password_reset_tokens(id, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, used_at, created_at).
- 1-hour expiry, single-use, sha256-hashed plaintext.

Flow:
- POST /api/auth/forgot {email} → 204 (always, regardless of whether email exists, to avoid enumeration). When the email matches a user with a local password set, mint a token and email a link to /reset?token=<plaintext>.
- POST /api/auth/reset {token, new_password} → 204 on success, 400 on expired/used/invalid. Updates password_hash + marks token used. Optional: invalidate other sessions for that user.

Frontend:
- 'Forgot password?' link on LoginView.
- Two new routes: /forgot (form) and /reset?token=… (form).
- Reuse PasswordInput + the existing register form's password validation.

Templates:
- Plain + HTML email body with the reset link and an explicit note that it expires.

Out of scope (still later):
- Rate limiting per email/IP.

## Context
Unblocked by bd-2vu (SMTP) which just landed. Holds the dependency arrow to indicate the order.
close_reason: shipped: POST /api/auth/forgot (always 204 — anti-enum) mints a sha256-hashed token, 60min expiry, single-use; emails a link via the SMTP infra from bd-2vu. POST /api/auth/reset validates len>=8, bcrypt-hashes new password, updates users.password_hash + marks token used_at in one tx. Frontend: /forgot, /reset routes + 'Forgot password?' link on LoginView. End-to-end verified: token consume → 204, reuse → 400, old password rejected, new password works.

title: Sidebar: add file and add folder buttons
description:
Two new buttons at the top of the ArchiveTree sidebar:

- **Add file**: opens a modal with path input (autocomplete from existing folders) + optional 'starter content' textarea. On submit: PUT /api/docs/<path> with the chosen body (or empty body for a fresh doc). The existing 404-create flow already does the work; this is just a friendlier entry point.
- **Add folder**: opens a modal asking for a folder path. Implementation choice: either drop a hidden `.gitkeep` (cleanest with git but visible in raw tree) OR defer until the user creates a file at that path (more pragmatic — empty folders generally aren't a thing in git anyway). Recommend skipping a separate folder action and instead letting 'Add file' accept nested paths like `docs/handbook/intro.md` so any missing intermediate folders are implied.

Both buttons gated by the 'write' permission on the destination path. Permission check happens server-side via the PUT route.

This lands BEFORE the template library ticket — once that exists, 'Add file' grows a 'from template' option.

## Context
User: 'There should be an add file and add folder button in the sidebar.' Plus future intent to wire in a template library.
close_reason: shipped: 'Add file' button in ArchiveTree sidebar (only for signed-in users). Modal accepts a nested path (folders implied) and optional initial body. Auto-navigates to the new doc. 'Add folder' deferred — nested paths cover the use case per the original ticket recommendation.

title: File actions in editor view: delete, move, copy
description:
Add three document-level actions, accessible only from the editor view (NOT the sidebar):

- **Delete**: removes the file, git rm + commit. Confirmation modal. After commit, redirect to README.md.
- **Move (rename)**: prompts for a new path. Re-checks safe-join, git mv + commit. Updates the comments table's path column (and re-anchors fresh sha). Re-indexes search. Navigates to the new path on success.
- **Copy**: prompts for a destination path. Creates a fresh file with a new git history (fresh frontmatter created/updated/version=1, copies body). Indexes the new file. No comments copied (orphaning is the user's call).

Each action sets git author + email to the logged-in user, matching the save flow. Each action is gated by the 'write' permission on the source path (and on the destination path for move/copy).

UI: a small overflow menu in the topbar's Edit mode actions (Carbon OverflowMenu) so the header stays uncluttered.

## Context
User wants destructive/rearranging file actions kept inside the editor (where you've already opened the thing), not in the sidebar. Pairs with the planned sidebar add-file/add-folder buttons (separate ticket).
close_reason: shipped: editor-area OverflowMenu with Move / Copy / Delete. Backend MoveFile/RemoveFile + comments.MovePath/DeletePath + indexer.Remove + auto-sync on each. Move auto-creates intermediate directories.

title: Enriched bot-ready output + read-only per-page sharing tokens
description:
Two combined needs:

1) Bot-ready output (doc §20): the /docs/<path>?botready=1 endpoint currently returns raw Markdown. Upgrade to the doc's spec:
   - text/markdown variant with a structured header (Path, Updated, Git SHA, Tags) and an 'Open Comments' section that lists open + relocated comments.
   - JSON variant via ?format=json with {path, title, frontmatter, markdown, html, comments, git: {sha, updated}}.

2) Per-page sharing tokens for read-only access:
   - Opaque token bound to a single document path + a single user (the creator). Inherits no other permissions; strictly read-only on that one path.
   - Token sent as Authorization: Bearer <token> OR ?token=<token>.
   - When set, the request is treated as that user for permission checks on JUST that path. Other reads/writes still 401/403.
   - Tokens are revocable from a small user-centric section (e.g. account settings / 'Sharing' tab) — list, create with note + optional expiry, revoke.
   - Storage: hashed (sha256) in DB so plaintext only shown once at creation.

Combining them so the bot-ready surface (the primary consumer of share links) lands with the token mechanism in one shippable iteration.

## Context
User requested combining the bot-ready enriched output with the sharing tokens. Tokens should be user-bound but strictly read-only; intended use case is sharing one page outside the org without granting broader access. References the existing bd-7u6 (general bot tokens for headless agents) as a sibling but with very different shape: this is per-user, per-page, read-only — bd-7u6 covers general API bearer tokens with scopes.
close_reason: shipped: enriched ?botready=1 markdown (Path, Updated, Git SHA, Version, Tags + Open Comments section) and ?botready=1&format=json variant (path/title/frontmatter/markdown/html/comments/git). Per-page share tokens land via the same api_tokens table using read:<path> scopes; UI in /account with a 'page share' radio that emits exactly read:<path>.

title: Bot tokens for headless agents
description:
Long-lived API tokens that bypass interactive auth so headless agents / CI / external automations can call /api endpoints. Per-token scopes (e.g. read-only, comments-only, full). Admin UI to mint, name, list, and revoke tokens. Sent as Authorization: Bearer <token>. Stored hashed in DB (similar to bcrypt or SHA-256 of the secret). Last-used timestamp on each successful auth.

## Context
M7 auth plan deferred this. Needed for the doc §20 bot-ready output to be usable on closed archives once anon-read is off.
close_reason: shipped: api_tokens table + Authorization: Bearer middleware + scope-aware authorize. Scopes: read/comment/write (each optionally :path). Account UI at /account mints, lists, revokes; plaintext shown once. Token-authenticated requests cannot mint further tokens.

title: Add SMTP integration for transactional email
description:
Required for password reset and email verification flows. Without SMTP, those auth flows are blocked. Should support standard SMTP credentials in config (host, port, username, password, from address, TLS toggle). Plain-text and HTML templates per flow.

## Context
Referenced from M7 auth plan (out-of-scope section) and doc §19. Filed as follow-up so it's tracked once we need password reset / verification.
close_reason: shipped: stdlib net/smtp transport with three encryption modes (none/starttls/tls), config block (email.host/port/username/password/encryption/from_address/from_name/reply_to/insecure_skip_verify), redacted status endpoint, 'send test email' admin button, Settings tab UI. No-op mailer when not configured so callers don't have to nil-check. Follow-ups filed as separate tickets for password reset + email verification.

title: Email verification on register / email change
description:
Now that SMTP infrastructure is in place (bd-2vu), require fresh accounts to confirm ownership of the email they used to register.

Schema:
- Add users.email_verified_at TEXT (nullable). NULL means not verified.
- New table email_verification_tokens(id, user_id, email, token_hash, expires_at, used_at, created_at). 24h expiry.

Flow:
- On register: send a verification email immediately. Account remains usable (no hard gate v1) but the UI shows a banner until verified.
- POST /api/auth/verify {token} → marks email_verified_at on success. Idempotent.
- On email change: invalidate verified_at, mint new token, send.

Frontend:
- Carbon InlineNotification on the doc views when me.email_verified_at is null, with a 'Resend verification' button.
- /verify?token=… route that calls the API and shows result.

Config knob:
- email.require_verification_for_writes: bool (default false). When true, writes/comments are gated until email_verified_at is set.

Out of scope:
- Bounce handling.

## Context
Unblocked by bd-2vu (SMTP). Lower priority than password reset since registered users can still operate; this only adds trust.
close_reason: shipped: POST /api/auth/verify (anon-OK) sha256-checks token, marks used_at, sets users.email_verified_at + COALESCEs the email column on the user. POST /api/auth/resend-verification (session-only, refuses token-auth) mints fresh token. Hook in Register sends initial verification email best-effort. Frontend: /verify route, unverified-email banner in CarbonShell, status + resend section in AccountView. End-to-end verified: verify → 204, reuse → 400, email_verified_at populated.

title: Plan: live config editing from the Settings tab
description:
Currently /admin → Settings shows config.yaml values read-only. The user has noted this needs design work before we ship a real editing surface.

Open questions to resolve in the planning session:
- Which fields are safely editable at runtime vs require a restart? (Server bind, DB path are restart-required; auth flags, anon-read, OIDC creds could potentially hot-swap.)
- How do we persist edits? Options:
  - Write back to config.yaml (operator's source of truth; raises file ownership / mode questions on multi-user hosts).
  - Mirror to DB; on next start, merge DB overrides on top of YAML defaults.
- How are sensitive fields (OIDC client_secret) handled — mask in UI, write-only, separate secret store?
- Validation + revert flow on bad input.
- Audit (whoever proposed the per-action audit table earlier: applies here regardless of git-as-audit decision since these are server-state edits, not doc edits).

Outcome: a written plan we can hand to an implementation session. Do not implement until this lands.

## Context
User: 'Live config editing needs more thoughts and should be stored as a to be planned session.' Captured here so the Settings tab placeholder isn't lost.
notes: Planning session complete. Full design lives at docs/plans/live-config-editing.md (committed to the repo). Locked decisions: hybrid YAML + DB overrides (config_overrides table); write-only sensitive fields with set/unset indicator; restart-required fields (server.bind, db.path, repo.path, repo.branch, frontend.embedded_dist) stay YAML-only and lock-iconned in UI; config_audit table for who/when/what edits. Architecture: new internal/configsvc package with Snapshot/Set/Unset/Subscribe; subsystems (mailer, OIDC, render) subscribe at boot and rebuild on relevant key changes. Sizing: ~600-800 LOC Go + ~400-600 LOC frontend. Implementation order documented in the plan; recommended first subsystem to wire for hot-reload is mailer (testable via existing 'send test email' button).
close_reason: shipped v1: hybrid YAML + DB overrides via new internal/configsvc package (Service with Snapshot/Set/Unset/Subscribe, redacted audit). Migration 009 adds config_overrides + config_audit. 7 Go tests in internal/configsvc/configsvc_test.go cover snapshot, set, unset, validation, subscribe-on-prefix, audit redaction, sensitive-value masking. Admin endpoints: GET schema/effective/audit/export, PATCH config. 35 fields registered (17 live-editable, 2 sensitive). Mailer subscribes to email.* and hot-rebuilds — verified end-to-end: PATCH email.host + email.from_address flips the mailer from disabled to enabled without restart. Auth.Service + API handlers read live snapshot for AllowAnonymousRead, LocalEnabled, repo.commit_author_*, auto_push, server.base_url. Frontend: ConfigEditor component in /admin Settings tab renders schema-driven form (Toggle/Select/NumberInput/PasswordInput/TextInput per type), per-field 'override' / 'yaml default' / 'configured' tags + revert button, recent edits DataTable, export-as-YAML download link. Deferred to follow-ups: OIDC live reload, render live reload, cross-field validation.

title: Comment replies via a simple parent reference
description:
Add a lightweight reply mechanism — NOT deep threads, NOT nested rendering trees. Just one level of relation so users can clarify or follow up on an existing comment.

Schema change:
- Add nullable `reply_to_id INTEGER REFERENCES comments(id) ON DELETE SET NULL` to comments.

API:
- POST /api/comments accepts an optional `reply_to` field.
- GET /api/comments?path=... includes `reply_to` in each comment.

UI:
- Sidebar groups by line as today; within a line group, replies render directly under their parent with a small indent (one level only — no recursion).
- 'Reply' button on every comment that opens AddCommentModal preset with the same line/anchor and the reply_to set.

Out of scope:
- Nested threading deeper than one parent → child level (a reply-to-a-reply still flat-points to the original comment).
- @mentions, markdown in comment bodies.

## Context
User: 'I am not sure about comment replies. Should just be another comment i don't need deep threads here. Maybe a related field in the db, but nothing more.'
close_reason: shipped: nullable reply_to_id on comments with ON DELETE SET NULL. comments.Create flattens a reply-to-a-reply onto the root (one-parent-only schema). API: /api/comments accepts optional reply_to; rejects parents from a different document. UI: 'Reply' button on every non-resolved comment dispatches a Svelte event; CarbonShell consumes it and opens AddCommentModal with the parent's line/anchor preset. CommentsSidebar groups by line, then root-first + replies under their parent, with a left-border indent + 'reply' tag. End-to-end verified: root id=2, reply id=3 reply_to=2, grandchild id=4 reply_to=2 (flattened).

title: Multi-factor authentication (MFA)
description:
TOTP at minimum (RFC 6238) with QR enrollment + backup codes. Optionally add WebAuthn / passkeys as a second factor. Per-user opt-in toggle in account settings; admin-level toggle to require MFA for role=admin (or all users). Recovery flow via backup codes or admin reset.

## Context
M7 auth plan deferred this. Worth doing after path permissions + bot tokens so we have a richer auth surface to protect.
close_reason: shipped: TOTP-only MFA. POST /api/auth/mfa/setup-start mints a fresh secret and returns a PNG QR (data: URI) + otpauth URL + base32 secret; setup-confirm flips totp_enabled_at after verifying a code. Login splits into two steps when MFA is enabled — password ok returns {mfa_required:true} and stashes mfa_pending_user_id in scs (half-auth); /api/auth/login/mfa consumes a TOTP code and rotates the session via RenewToken. Disable requires a current code (both user-side and admin-emergency endpoints). Admin: Users table grows a 2FA column + 'Disable 2FA' emergency unlock. End-to-end verified: setup → confirm → logout → login {mfa_required:true} → /api/me 204 → login/mfa → /api/me 200 → PUT 200.

title: OIDC ↔ local account linking by email
description:
On OIDC callback, if a local user already exists with the same email address, link oidc_provider/oidc_subject to that existing row instead of creating a duplicate user. Simple match — exact email comparison, case-insensitive normalisation. Edge cases: email already linked to a different OIDC subject (refuse with clear error), local user has unverified email (still link, document trust assumption).

## Context
Per the M7 auth plan, v1 keeps OIDC users and local users separate even when emails match. This is the next-iteration enhancement.
close_reason: shipped: EnsureFromOIDC now links to existing local users by normalized email when no OIDC binding exists. Mismatch (email already bound to a different provider+subject) returns ErrOIDCMismatch; OIDC callback redirects to /login?oidc_error=mismatch with a translated banner. Five Go tests in internal/users/users_test.go cover create-new, idempotent, link-by-email, refuse-mismatch, case-insensitive email.

title: Template library from a public git repo
description:
When creating a new doc via the sidebar's 'Add file' flow, offer a 'from template' option that pulls starter content from a configurable public git repo.

Behaviour sketch:
- Config: `templates.git_url` (public HTTPS git URL), `templates.path` (optional subpath in the repo). Optional: `templates.refresh_interval` for caching.
- On 'from template', the modal shows a Carbon `StructuredList` (or `Tile`s) of available templates. Each template is a markdown file in the configured repo; the template's filename becomes the label, its content is the starter body.
- A manifest file (`templates.yaml`) at the repo root can override labels/descriptions/categories — optional.
- Server-side: a small templates package that does a shallow clone (or sparse fetch) on first request, caches in memory + on disk, refreshes on a TTL.
- No auth needed (public repo). For private template repos, defer to a follow-up.

## Context
User's afterthought during the auth wrap-up — file for the future, low priority. Concept is to lean on git's existing distribution model for templates instead of building a custom registry.
